download_files.access_note: No login required, but CSV is generated via a Shiny session download (POST to a session-specific URL, not a stable direct link). User must navigate to Quick Search or Advanced Search tab and click 'Download as CSV'. No persistent URL exists for the full dataset. Confirmed functional via HTTP 200 on homepage (x-powered-by: Shiny Server).
download_files.description: Full metadata dataset: up to 69,822 rows × 203 columns. Downloadable from Quick Search tab without applying any filter. This is the primary metadata file for BioThings ingestion. The 'Download as CSV' button with no filters applied exports the entire dataset.
download_files.stability: session-bound — URL changes per session; not a static file URL

download_files.access_note: GitHub repository is public, no auth required. Script downloads raw FASTQ/FASTA sequencing files (not metadata). Input is the CSV metadata export from the web app.
download_files.description: Python3 downloader script. Downloads raw sequencing data from SRA (via EBI FTP: ftp://ftp.sra.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/fastq/) and MG-RAST (via HTTPS API: https://api.metagenomics.anl.gov/1/download/). Aspera also supported. NOT a metadata downloader — used for raw sequences after metadata CSV is obtained.
download_files.ingestion_relevance: LOW — raw sequences not needed for BioThings metadata plugin. The metadata CSV from the web app is the relevant artifact.

schema.source: Paper (PMC7778935, DOI: 10.1093/nar/gkaa1031) + PMC full text + Advanced Search tab category structure

fields.name: run_id
fields.note: SRA Run ID — primary key. Prefixes: SRR (NCBI), ERR (ENA), DRR (DDBJ)

fields.name: MG-RAST_metagenome_ID
fields.note: Only for MG-RAST-sourced samples (4.6% of records)

fields.name: sex
fields.note: Standardized: female/male

fields.name: age
fields.note: In years; converted from days/weeks/months

fields.name: age_class
fields.note: Derived: infant (<3), child (3-10), teenager (10-16), adult (16-50), elder (>50)

fields.name: body_mass_index
fields.note: BMI; categories: underweight to obese

fields.name: ethnicity
fields.note: Standardized to U.S. Census OMB categories: White, Black or African American, Asian, American Indian or Alaska Native, Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

fields.name: diagnosis
fields.note: Aggregated diagnosis column; 58 distinct values; standardized using Human Disease Ontology terms

fields.name: HMgDB_control
fields.note: Identifies which disease study a control sample belongs to

fields.name: [diagnosis_disease_columns]
fields.note: One binary column per diagnosis (58 total): e.g., colon_cancer, crohns_disease, parkinsons_disease, cancer — positive or negative per sample

fields.name: sample_site
fields.note: Hierarchical; standardized via BRENDA Tissue Ontology. 9 main categories: gut, oral, skin, ear, lung, vagina, bone, bio-fluids, and sub-categories

fields.name: diet
fields.note: Standardized: Vegetarian, Vegan, Omnivore

fields.name: country
fields.note: Standardized to ISO 3166-1; 59 countries

fields.name: latitude
fields.note: Decimal Degrees format

fields.name: longitude
fields.note: Decimal Degrees format

fields.name: sequencing_platform
fields.note: Available in SRA metadata; 94.3% Illumina

fields.name: average_read_length_bp
fields.note: Computed as basepairs_count / sequence_count; samples >600 bp avg classified as assembled

fields.name: sequence_count
fields.note: Available in SRA metadata

fields.name: basepairs_count
fields.note: Available in SRA metadata

fields.name: quality_above_30
fields.note: SRA-Tinder quality score — not in standard SRA metadata

fields.name: drisee_score_raw
fields.note: MG-RAST quality metric — MG-RAST samples only

fields.name: library_creation_date
fields.note: Retrieved via Entrez Direct; ISO 8601 format

primary_key.note: Globally unique INSDC run accession. Used as _id in BioThings document. MG-RAST records use metagenome_id as fallback (4.6% of records).

schema.schema_confidence_note: Field categories and representative names from PMC full text (DOI: 10.1093/nar/gkaa1031) and the Advanced Search UI categories. Exact column header names in the CSV export (case, underscores vs spaces) could not be confirmed without a live CSV download — the Shiny app does not expose a direct download URL. Actual CSV column names must be verified at plugin-build time.

license.observed_site: CC BY (observed via © 2024 footer; no explicit license URL on homepage)
license.note: Paper states CC-BY permitting unrestricted reuse with attribution. No NC or ND restrictions. License version not pinned (CC BY, not explicitly 4.0) but context consistent with CC BY 4.0.

plugin_inputs.data_url_note: No stable bulk download URL exists. Data is accessed via the Shiny web app's 'Download as CSV' button in the Quick Search tab (no filter applied = full dataset). A Selenium or Playwright headless browser step is required to automate the download for the BioThings dumper. Alternatively, contact authors for a direct data file — the GitHub downloader script (hmgdb_downloader.py) downloads raw FASTQ files, not metadata CSVs.
plugin_inputs.ingestion_rationale: HYBRID: The metadata CSV must be obtained via Shiny session download (no stable URL — requires browser automation or one-time manual download). The GitHub script downloads raw sequences, not metadata. Once the CSV is obtained, it is self-contained for BioThings ingestion — no API crawl needed for metadata. The 'hybrid' classification reflects the non-standard download mechanism (browser-driven CSV export) rather than a traditional API + bulk download combination.
plugin_inputs.recommended_dumper_strategy: One-time manual download of full CSV from Quick Search tab (no filter) to a stable hosted location (e.g. Zenodo or S3), then point the BioThings dumper at that stable URL. Or implement a Playwright/Selenium dumper that opens the Shiny app and triggers the CSV download.

paper_vs_reality.note: Count unchanged since v1.1 release. Planned biannual updates (Feb and Sep) appear to have lapsed — no evidence of updates since June 2020.

paper_vs_reality.observed: 580 — stated on homepage consistent with paper
203 — stated consistently on homepage and confirmed in paper full text. Individual column names not fully enumerable without live CSV download.
58 — paper states 58; homepage consistent. Cannot confirm independently without CSV access.
59 — paper (Results section) states 59 countries; abstract states 58. Minor internal inconsistency in paper itself.
paper_vs_reality.note: MISMATCH within paper: abstract says 58 countries, Results section says 59. Relevancy report said 58. Cannot verify current count without CSV.

paper_vs_reality.observed: Confirmed from paper full text. SRA: 66,589 samples; MG-RAST: remainder.
MISMATCH: Canonical URL in paper is https://webapp.ufz.de/hmgdb/ but current live URL is https://web.app.ufz.de/hmgdb/. Both respond with HTTP 200. The web.app.ufz.de domain is current.
paper_vs_reality.note: URL migration occurred post-publication. Both domains may resolve, but web.app.ufz.de is the active one (confirmed 2026-06-29).

paper_vs_reality.claim: Biannual updates: first week February and first week September
paper_vs_reality.observed: MISMATCH: Database still shows Release 1.1, June 2020. No updates observed since v1.1. Over 6 years without update as of inspection date (2026-06-29). Update cadence has clearly lapsed.
paper_vs_reality.note: HIGH RISK for staleness. 69,822 records reflect 2020 snapshot; thousands of new human metagenomes have been deposited in SRA since then.

paper_vs_reality.claim: No REST API — web Shiny interface only
paper_vs_reality.observed: Confirmed. No API endpoints found. Shiny Server confirmed via x-powered-by header. No /api/ routes respond with data.

paper_vs_reality.claim: GitHub script for raw sequence download (hmgdb_downloader.py)
paper_vs_reality.observed: Confirmed. Script is public at https://github.com/mdsufz/hmgdb_script. Downloads FASTQ from EBI SRA FTP and MG-RAST HTTPS. Input is the metadata CSV from the web app. No metadata download functionality in the script itself.

paper_vs_reality.claim: License: Creative Commons Attribution License (CC BY)
paper_vs_reality.observed: Consistent with paper — homepage shows © 2024 footer. No explicit CC BY badge on current site, but no contradictory license observed.

risks.description: No stable bulk download URL — data only accessible via Shiny session
risks.detail: The 'Download as CSV' button in the Shiny web app generates a session-bound download. There is no permanent /download or /export URL that returns the full dataset. Standard BioThings dumper patterns (wget/curl of a file URL) will not work. The GitHub script (hmgdb_downloader.py) downloads raw FASTQ sequences, not the metadata CSV.
risks.mitigation: Option 1 (recommended): Contact authors (UFZ / ICMC USP) to request a direct metadata dump file or a stable hosted URL. Option 2: Implement a Playwright/Selenium-based dumper that opens the Shiny app, clicks 'Download as CSV' with no filters, and captures the downloaded file. Option 3: Request that authors deposit the metadata CSV on Zenodo under the CC-BY license.

risks.description: Database not updated since June 2020 — 6+ year staleness
risks.detail: Homepage shows 'Release 1.1 (June 2020)' as of inspection date 2026-06-29. The paper promised biannual updates (February and September) but no updates have occurred since v1.1. Thousands of new human WGS metagenomes have been deposited in SRA since 2020. The 69,822 records represent an increasingly stale snapshot.
risks.mitigation: Contact authors before committing to ingestion to determine whether the database is still maintained. If not, evaluate whether the 2020 snapshot is still scientifically valuable. Consider PRIME or GMrepo as alternative sources with active maintenance.

risks.description: Exact CSV column headers unconfirmed — schema is inferred from paper
risks.detail: The 203 field names are documented in the paper's supplementary tables (S1-S6) but these are not freely accessible (OUP supplementary ZIP returned 403 Forbidden). Column names in the actual CSV export (casing, spacing, special characters) could not be verified without a live download. The plugin transformer will need to map whatever column headers appear in the actual CSV.
risks.mitigation: Perform a manual test download from the web app during plugin development. The first step of the plugin build should be to download a sample CSV and print/log the exact header row before writing any transformation logic.

risks.description: Diagnosis fields use free-text ontology — no MONDO/ICD identifiers
risks.detail: The 58 diagnoses are standardized using Human Disease Ontology (HDO) terms but stored as human-readable strings, not HDO numeric IDs. Per-disease binary columns (positive/negative) are included but disease names require normalization to standard ontology IDs before BioThings integration. The 203 attributes include ~58 per-disease columns that are mostly null per sample.
risks.mitigation: Add a post-processing step in the plugin uploader to map diagnosis strings to MONDO IDs using the MONDO OBO (available via OLS or BioPortal). Filter null/empty binary disease columns at ingest time.

risks.description: High sparsity — most metadata fields are study-specific and null for most samples
risks.detail: Attributes such as diet, pets_presence, contraceptives_use, and most exposure fields are only populated for samples from studies that specifically collected this information. With 203 columns and 69,822 rows, the matrix is highly sparse. Most per-sample documents will have only ~20-30 non-null fields out of 203.
risks.mitigation: Filter null/empty values at ingest time per the BioThings plugin convention. Only store non-null fields in the document. This reduces document size and improves query performance.

risks.description: Shiny app dependency creates ingestion fragility
risks.detail: The entire data access path depends on the Shiny web application at web.app.ufz.de/hmgdb/ remaining available and functional. If UFZ decommissions or migrates the app (as evidenced by the URL change from webapp.ufz.de to web.app.ufz.de already having occurred post-publication), the data becomes inaccessible. No alternative dump location is known.
risks.mitigation: At first successful download, immediately deposit the full metadata CSV to a stable archive (Zenodo or S3) and point the BioThings dumper at that archive URL. Build version tracking into the plugin.

risks.description: URL migration from webapp.ufz.de to web.app.ufz.de
risks.detail: Paper and Data Availability Statement list https://webapp.ufz.de/hmgdb/ but current live site is https://web.app.ufz.de/hmgdb/. The old URL may or may not redirect. Any hardcoded URL in the plugin must use the new domain.
risks.mitigation: Use https://web.app.ufz.de/hmgdb/ in all plugin code. Test whether old URL redirects. Document both URLs in plugin metadata.

risks.description: Aspera requirement in GitHub downloader script for raw sequences
risks.detail: The Aspera-accelerated download path in hmgdb_downloader.py requires the ascp binary and an Aspera SSH key. Standard HTTPS FTP (EBI SRA ftp://ftp.sra.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/fastq/) is available as the default path. The ascp path is optional for speed. This is only relevant if raw FASTQ files are needed (not for metadata-only plugin).
risks.mitigation: Not relevant for a metadata-only BioThings plugin. No action needed unless raw sequence download integration is required.

risks.description: Country count discrepancy within the paper itself
risks.detail: The paper abstract states 58 countries; the paper's Results section states 59 countries. The relevancy report used 58. The actual count in the CSV cannot be confirmed without a live download.
risks.mitigation: Accept whichever value appears in the downloaded CSV. Do not hard-code expected country counts in the plugin.
